clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Guideline-directed medical therapy for patients with comorbid Hypertension and Heart Failure with reduced Ejection Fraction
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: HFrEF diagnosis with NYHA class II-IV symptoms, regardless of hypertension status
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: Recommendation of 4 foundational drug classes (BB, MRA, RAASi/ARNi, SGLT2i) plus adjunctive therapy

navigationPath.purpose: Entry point for GDMT recommendation engine
Confirm HFrEF diagnosis and NYHA class
Input eGFR and potassium levels for MRA eligibility
Check type 2 diabetes status for SGLT2i
Determine if patient self-identifies as Black for hydralazine/ISDN
Assess intolerance to first-line agents (ARNi, ACEi, ARB)

firstLineIntolerance.description: Intolerance to ARNi, ACEi, or ARB

validationRules: NYHA class II-III for ARNi preference over ACEi/ARB

rules: Rule 1: Beta-blocker (BB) - Recommend bisoprolol, carvedilol, or metoprolol succinate for all HFrEF patients, even if asymptomatic
Rule 2: MRA - Recommend spironolactone or eplerenone if symptomatic HFrEF AND eGFR >30 AND potassium <5.0
Rule 3: RAASi/ARNi - For NYHA II-III, ARNi preferred over ACEi/ARB. If ARNi not feasible, use ACEi or ARB
Rule 4: SGLT2i - Recommend for symptomatic HFrEF regardless of diabetes status
Rule 5: Hydralazine/ISDN - For Black patients with NYHA III-IV on optimal therapy, OR for any patient with intolerance to first-line agents
Rule 6: All 4 foundational classes (BB + MRA + RAASi/ARNi + SGLT2i) should be considered as GDMT

drugClasses.condition: Symptomatic HFrEF AND eGFR >30 AND K+ <5.0
HFrEF with NYHA II-III - ARNi preferred
Black patients with NYHA III-IV on optimal therapy OR patients with intolerance to first-line agents
drugClasses.benefit: Improves symptoms, reduces morbidity and mortality

recommendedDrugClasses.description: Array of drug classes to prescribe

priorityOrder.description: Order of addition if not on all agents

conditionalRecommendations.description: e.g., Hydralazine/ISDN based on race or intolerance

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: Start with BB + RAASi/ARNi, add MRA and SGLT2i based on eligibility, consider hydralazine/ISDN for specific populations
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Recheck eGFR and potassium within 2-4 weeks of MRA initiation, monitor BP and symptoms monthly
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: Reassess NYHA class every 3-6 months, recheck renal function quarterly
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Alert if patient qualifies for SGLT2i or ARNi but not prescribed
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log all GDMT agents prescribed, reasons for omission if any
